description: Evaluates cryptographic key management maturity—service governance, KPIs/KRIs, encryption policies, HSM and cloud KMS platforms, CEK roles, key provisioning and lifecycle operations, revocation and compromise response, and machine identity key governance.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: A cryptographic key management service exists, is scoped appropriately, and is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise cryptographic key management service exists
Regular cryptographic key management service efficacy review

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Cryptographic key management KPIs and KRIs—including rotation compliance, HSM/cloud KMS coverage, and orphaned key metrics—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Documented KPIs and KRIs
Regular KPI/KRI review
Key rotation and expiry compliance metrics
HSM and cloud KMS coverage metrics
Orphaned and unknown key inventory metrics

capabilities.name: Encryption and key management policy
capabilities.description: Enterprise-wide cryptographic procedures and key provisioning and deprovisioning policies are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise-wide cryptographic procedures and policies
Enterprise-wide key provisioning policies
Enterprise-wide key deprovisioning policies
Regular policy and procedure review

capabilities.name: Key inventory and KMS/HSM platform
capabilities.description: A key management solution or cloud KMS with HSM backing manages cryptographic keys with automation, secure storage, configuration review, and integrations.
evaluation_focus: Key management solution or cloud KMS for cryptographic keys
HSM or hardware-backed key protection for high-value keys
Automation support for enterprise keys
Regular key management solution configuration review
Integration with data management and certificate management
Secure storage of cryptographic keys

capabilities.name: CEK roles and responsibilities
capabilities.description: Clear roles and responsibilities exist for cryptographic key escrow and CEK management.
evaluation_focus: Clear CEK management roles and responsibilities

capabilities.name: Key provisioning and deprovisioning
capabilities.description: Key provisioning and deprovisioning processes are formalized, automated, and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Formalized documented key provisioning process
Formalized documented key deprovisioning process
Automated key provisioning
Automated key deprovisioning
Regular key provisioning and deprovisioning process review

capabilities.name: Key rotation
capabilities.description: A formalized key rotation process supports automatic rotation and is reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Formalized documented key rotation process
Automatic key rotation
Regular key rotation process review

capabilities.name: Key lifecycle state management
capabilities.description: Key activation, deactivation, suspension, archival, and recovery processes are enterprise-wide, support automation, and are reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise-wide key activation and deactivation processes
Automatic key activation from change management triggers
Enterprise-wide key suspension process
Key suspension based on compromise triggers
Enterprise-wide key archival and recovery policies
Automatic key archival and recovery
Regular lifecycle state process review

capabilities.name: Key revocation and compromise response
capabilities.description: Key revocation and compromise response processes are enterprise-wide, automated on triggers, and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise-wide key revocation process and procedures
Automated key revocation based on triggers
Enterprise-wide key compromise policies
Remove or deprovision keys when compromised
Regular key revocation and compromise process review

capabilities.name: Cloud KMS and machine identity key governance
capabilities.description: Cloud KMS, BYOK/HYOK, and workload key lifecycle are governed with machine identity attribution and separation from human-operated keys.
evaluation_focus: Governed cloud KMS usage across cloud providers
BYOK and HYOK policies for cloud encryption
Workload and service key lifecycle in cloud KMS
Separation of human-administered and machine-managed keys

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
